chunks.text: Stereo. HC JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Murder Reference No.04 of 2018 (The State Vs. Muhammad Fayyaz) Criminal Appeal No. 70 of 2018 (Muhammad Fayyaz Vs. The State.) J U D G M E N T Date of hearing: 07.12.2021. Appellant by: Mr. Muhammad Umair Mohsin, Advocate. State by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. Complainant by: Syed Zeeshan Haider, Advocate. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J.– Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta (convict) was tried along with Muhammad Amir, Muhammad Ijaz, Sajjad Ahmad, Muhammad Dilshad and Allah Ditta ( all since acquitted) by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Ahmadpur East in the case FIR No. 113 of 2016 dated 23.07.2016 registered at Police Station Noshehra Jadeed, District Bahawalpur in respect of offences under sections 302,148 and 149 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Ghulam Yasin son of Khuda Bakhsh (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 25.01.2018, convicted Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta (convict) and sentenced him as under: Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 2 Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta: Death under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Ghulam Yasin son of Khuda Bakhsh (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.200,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. The convict namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta was extended the benefit available under Section 382-B of Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 by the learned trial court.

The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. The convict namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta was extended the benefit available under Section 382-B of Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 by the learned trial court. Muhammad Amir, Muhammad Ijaz, Sajjad Ahmad, Muhammad Dilshad and Allah Ditta, the co-accused of the convict, were however acquitted by the learned trial court. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta (convict) lodged the Criminal Appeal No.70 of 2018 assailing his conviction and sentence. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.04 of 2018 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta. We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 70 of 2018 and Murder Reference No.04 of 2018 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as narrated by Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3), the complainant of the case are as under:- “On 23.07.2016, just before Maghrib, Asghar, Akhtar and I were sitting at a nearby 'dera' and were having conversation. Meanwhile Ghulam Yaseen, after ploughing the fields, was returning on the road by tractor and was heading towards his house. When Ghulam Yaseen reached near the house Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 3 of Allah Ditta, situated in a street, accused Fayaz armed with pistol, Amir armed with pistol., Sajjad armed with hatchet, Ejaz armed with 'soti', Dilshad armed with 'soti' and Allah Ditta armed with hatchet surrounded the tractor of Ghulam Yaseen. Allah Ditta raised 'lalkara' by stating that Ghulam Yaseen would not be spared. Amir made fire with his pistol towards Ghulam Yaseen but the fire was missed. Fayaz by climbing on the frame, fixed behind the tractor, fired upon Ghulam Yaseen which hit on his neck.

Amir made fire with his pistol towards Ghulam Yaseen but the fire was missed. Fayaz by climbing on the frame, fixed behind the tractor, fired upon Ghulam Yaseen which hit on his neck. The tractor stopped after covering a short distance. Akhtar and I attended Ghulam Yaseen. The people of locality started gathering there and the accused decamped after making firing. Then we shifted Ghulam Yaseen to Bahawalpur after making arrangements of the transport. GhulamYaseen breathed his last and we transported his dead body to RHC Mubarakpur. Police came there and I preferred application Ex.PC which contains my thumb impression The motive behind this occurrence was that the deceased Ghulam Yaseen was my son and I have a tubewell there. Accused Allah Dittal and others had purchased tubewell water for irrigation and Ghulam Yaseen had gone there to demand the money from them on 22.07.2016 where verbal altercation ensued. Due to this grudge, the accused had committed the occurrence. All the accused are present in the court today.” 4. After the formal investigation of the case report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 was submitted before the learned trial court wherein the accused were sent to face trial. The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 06.04.2017, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. The prosecution in order to prove its case got statements of as many as eleven witnesses recorded. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4). Muhammad Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 4 Aslam, ASI (PW-1) stated that on 23.07.2016 he recorded the formal F.I.R (Exh.PA). Muhammad Fayyaz 1723/C (PW-2) stated that on 23.07.2016 he escorted the dead body of the deceased to the hospital for the purpose of post mortem examination and also received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Medical Officer.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 4 Aslam, ASI (PW-1) stated that on 23.07.2016 he recorded the formal F.I.R (Exh.PA). Muhammad Fayyaz 1723/C (PW-2) stated that on 23.07.2016 he escorted the dead body of the deceased to the hospital for the purpose of post mortem examination and also received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Medical Officer. Muhammad Aslam, Patwari (PW-5) stated that he prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PJ). Abdul Rasheed 1500/MHC (PW-6) stated that on 23.07.2016 Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7) handed over to him one sealed parcel said to contain blood stained earth and one sealed parcel said to contain two empties and on 27.09.2016 he handed over the said sealed parcels to Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Abdul Rasheed 1500/MHC (PW-6) further stated that on 30.10.2016 Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7) handed over to him two sealed parcels said to contain a pistol which on 29.12.2016 he handed over the said sealed parcel to Abdul Razaq, ASI (PW-8) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Muhammad Asif, ASI (PW-11) stated that he had been posted with Mulazim Hussain, SI (not produced as he was suffering from paralysis) and recognized the signature of Mulazim Hussain, SI on the oral statement (Exh.PC) of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3). Shamas-ud- Din, SI (PW-7) investigated the case from 23.07.2016 till 05.09.2017, arrested the appellant on 19.10.2016, and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 5 6. The prosecution also got Dr. Muhammad Nauman (PW-10) examined, who on 24.07.2016 was posted as Medical Officer at RHC Mubarakpur and on the same day conducted the postmortem examination of the dead body of Ghulam Yasin son of Khuda Bakhsh (deceased). Dr. Muhammad Nauman (PW-10),on examining the dead body of Ghulam Yasin son of Khuda Bakhsh (deceased) observed as under:- “DESCRIPTION OF INJURIES.( Including findings on dissection): 1. 01 x 01 cm radius pointed hole with inverted margins on left side of neck.No blackening seen.

Dr. Muhammad Nauman (PW-10),on examining the dead body of Ghulam Yasin son of Khuda Bakhsh (deceased) observed as under:- “DESCRIPTION OF INJURIES.( Including findings on dissection): 1. 01 x 01 cm radius pointed hole with inverted margins on left side of neck.No blackening seen. No tattooing seen. Suppose to be point of entry of bullet. 2. Pointed hole 1.5 cm x 01cm radius on right side of neck, with averted margins. Bleeding suppose to be point of exit of bullet...................... FINAL OPINION. After performing complete and thorough autopsy of the dead body, found ruptured blood vessels of the neck by fire arm due to which all body cavities were flooded with blood and blood was drained out of the vessels. According to my opinion said injuries were ante mortem in nature and such type of injuries were sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of life. However, result will be KUO for Histopathologist report and consider following initial as provisional statement. After receiving the report of Histpathology from Punjab forensic Science Agency, have come to conclusion that cause of death was same as already mentioned above.” 7. On 21.12.2017 the learned Deputy District Public Prosecutor gave up the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Asghar as being unnecessary and closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PQ ) regarding the analysis of the blood stained earth and the report of Punjab Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 6 Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PR) regarding the analysis of the empties and the pistol (P-8) recovered from the possession of the appellant. 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellant namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, he replied that he had been involved in the case falsely and was innocent.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 6 Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PR) regarding the analysis of the empties and the pistol (P-8) recovered from the possession of the appellant. 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellant namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, he replied that he had been involved in the case falsely and was innocent. The appellant namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta opted not to get himself examined under section 340 Cr.P.C. and did not adduce any evidence in his defence. 9. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Ahmadpur East convicted and sentenced the appellant as referred to above. 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellant precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible, and relevant evidence. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the statements of the witnesses, on the face of it, is highly improbable. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the statements of the prosecution witnesses were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellant also submitted that the recovery of the pistol (P-8) was full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value, and result of fake proceedings. The learned counsel for the appellant also argued that the appellant had been involved in the occurrence due to enmity. The learned Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 7 counsel for the appellant finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 7 counsel for the appellant finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses. They further argued that the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellant. They further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4). They further argued that the recovery of the pistol (P-8) from the appellant namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta also corroborated the ocular account. They further contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offender with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant prayed for the rejection of appeal as lodged by the appellant namely Muhammad Fayyaz son of Allah Ditta. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellant, the learned counsel for the complainant, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and with their able assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial., 13. The whole prosecution case revolves around the statements of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4), the eye witnesses of the occurrence. The relationship of both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) with the deceased Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 8 and each other is on record. Ghulam Yaseen (deceased) was the real son of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) whereas the son of paternal cousin (Chachazad) of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4).According to the admission of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) himself, he was not the resident of the place of occurrence or anywhere around it.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 8 and each other is on record. Ghulam Yaseen (deceased) was the real son of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) whereas the son of paternal cousin (Chachazad) of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4).According to the admission of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) himself, he was not the resident of the place of occurrence or anywhere around it. The occurrence in this case took place on a road laid in front of the house of one Allah Ditta and the said place of occurrence was surrounded by the lands of Fida Hussain, Bashir Aheer and Hafiz Rasheed. The said Allah Ditta, Fida Hussain, Bashir Aheer and Hafiz Rasheed never joined the investigation of the case nor appeared as witnesses before the learned trial court. Nonetheless, Khuda Bakhsh (PW- 3) admitted that his residence was at a distance of one kilometre from the place of occurrence. Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) during cross-examination stated as under:- “The deceased was my son, PW Akhtar is my "Chachazad' and PW Asghar is my 'Mamoonzad'. The house of PW Asghar is at a distance of one kilometer from the place of occurrence..................... Asghar PW and I reside at the same place.................... On the east of the place of occurrence is house of Fida Hussain and there were crops of Bashir Aheer and Hafiz Rasheed near the place of occurrence.”(emphasis supplied) Though Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) admitted that he was not a resident of any place at or around the place of occurrence however at the same time both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 9 Akhtar (PW-4) stated that the house of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) was near the place of occurrence and for the reason that the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were present inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4), therefore, they witnessed the occurrence. This claim of the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) remained not proved rather it was proved to be a false claim.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 9 Akhtar (PW-4) stated that the house of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) was near the place of occurrence and for the reason that the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were present inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4), therefore, they witnessed the occurrence. This claim of the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) remained not proved rather it was proved to be a false claim. We have perused the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PJ) as prepared by Muhammad Aslam Patwari (PW-5) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PM) as prepared by Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, and find that the claimed house/Dera/Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) has not been shown in the said site plans (Exh.PJ and Exh.PM). Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, claimed that he had prepared the said site plan (Exh.PM) on the pointing of the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) and had any house/Dera/Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) existed at the place of occurrence, then the existence of the same would have been reflected in the said site plans (Exh.PJ and Exh.PM). This omission is conspicuous and has far reaching repercussions. It was the claim of both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) that they had witnessed the occurrence when they were present inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) and did not claim that they had moved from the said place of their presence at the time of the occurrence. When both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were claiming that they were present inside the Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 10 Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4), then the non-mentioning of the said Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) in the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PJ) as prepared by Muhammad Aslam Patwari (PW-5) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PM) as prepared by Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, which proved that no such house/Dera/Baithak existed at or around the place of occurrence and therefore the same was not reflected in the said site plans (Exh.PJ and Exh.PM), means that both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) made a false claim as to the place of their presence at the time of occurrence.

When both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were claiming that they were present inside the Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 10 Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4), then the non-mentioning of the said Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) in the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PJ) as prepared by Muhammad Aslam Patwari (PW-5) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PM) as prepared by Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, which proved that no such house/Dera/Baithak existed at or around the place of occurrence and therefore the same was not reflected in the said site plans (Exh.PJ and Exh.PM), means that both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) made a false claim as to the place of their presence at the time of occurrence. Moreover, both Muhammad Aslam Patwari (PW-5) and Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, admitted during cross- examination that they had not mentioned the names of the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) in the site plans (Exh.PJ and Exh.PM) prepared by them. Muhammad Aslam Patwari (PW-5) during cross-examination stated as under:- “I prepared the scaled site plan according to the instructions given to me by the police and that is why the names of any of the accused, complainant and witnesses are not mentioned in it. ” Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, during cross-examination stated as under:- “It is correct that site plan Ex.PM does not contain the name of any of the witnesses. It is correct that at all the points mentioned in Ex.PM, the name of none of the accused is mentioned in them” Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 11 Another aspect drawing our grave concern is the fact that both the prosecution witnesses made blatant improvements to their previous statements with regard to the place of their presence at the time of occurrence. It has been brought on record that neither in the oral statement (Exh.PC) of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) nor in the statement recorded under section 161 Cr.P.C. (Exh.DA) it was recorded that both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were present inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) when they witnessed the occurrence. Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) was cross-examined in this regard and the learned trial court observed as under:- “I do not remember whether in my application Ex.PC I had mentioned that I alongwith Asghar and Akhtar was sitting at a nearby 'dera'.

It has been brought on record that neither in the oral statement (Exh.PC) of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) nor in the statement recorded under section 161 Cr.P.C. (Exh.DA) it was recorded that both the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were present inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) when they witnessed the occurrence. Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) was cross-examined in this regard and the learned trial court observed as under:- “I do not remember whether in my application Ex.PC I had mentioned that I alongwith Asghar and Akhtar was sitting at a nearby 'dera'. Confronted with Ex.PC wherein it is not so recorded. ”(emphasis supplied). Similarly, Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) was also duly confronted with his previous statement with regard to the place of presence of the witnesses prior to the occurrence and the learned trial court observed as under:- “I had got recorded my statement to the police. In my statement recorded u/s 161 Cr.P.C I had mentioned that Khuda Bakhsh, Asghar and I were sitting outside my 'Baithak'. Confronted with Ex.DA wherein the word Batihak is not recorded. ”(emphasis supplied) The above referred portion of the cross-examination of the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) clearly reflects that both the prosecution witnesses were conscious of the fact that they were not the residents of anywhere at or around the place of Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 12 occurrence, therefore, they introduced the above mentioned blatant improvement in their statements that they were present inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) at the time of occurrence. As mentioned above, the fact that Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) had any house/Dera/Baithak or even any land near the place of occurrence, was not proved. The said improvements made by Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were substantial and were made with regard to crucial aspects of the prosecution evidence. By improving their previous statements, Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) impeached their own credit. Article 151 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat Order 1984 provides as under: - “151. Impeaching credit of witness.

Article 151 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat Order 1984 provides as under: - “151. Impeaching credit of witness. The credit of a witness may be impeached in the following ways by the adverse party, or, with the consent of the Court, by the party who calls him: by the evidence of persons who testify that they, from their knowledge of the witness, believe him to be un worthy of credit; by proof that the witness has been bribed, or has accepted the offer of a bribe, or has received any other corrupt inducement to give his evidence; by proof of former statements inconsistent with any part of his evidence which is liable to be contradicted;” As Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) introduced dishonest, blatant and substantial improvements to their previous statements and were duly confronted with their former statements, hence their credit stands impeached and Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) cannot be relied upon on being proved to have deposed with a slight, intended to mislead the court. The august Supreme Court of Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 13 Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Ashraf Vs. State” (2012 SCMR 419) took a serious notice of the improvements introduced by witnesses and rejected their evidence. We, thus, are satisfied that the evidence of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) has no intrinsic worth and is to be rejected out rightly. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in a recent case reported as “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Once the Court comes to the conclusion that the eye witnesses had made dishonest improvements in their statements then it is not safe to place reliance on their statements.

The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in a recent case reported as “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Once the Court comes to the conclusion that the eye witnesses had made dishonest improvements in their statements then it is not safe to place reliance on their statements. It is also settled by this Court that whenever a witness made dishonest improvement in his version in order to bring his case in line with the medical evidence or in order to strengthen the prosecution case then his testimony is not worthy of credence”. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case reported as Muhammad Arif Vs. The State (2019 SCMR 631) has enunciated the following principle: “It is well established by now that when a witness improves his statement and moment it is observed that the said improvement was made dishonestly to strengthen the prosecution, such portion of his statement is to be discarded out of consideration. Having observed the improvements in the statements of both the witnesses of ocular account, we hold that it is not safe to rely on their testimony to maintain conviction and sentence of Muhammad Arif (appellant) on a capital charge.” Guidance is sought from the principle enunciated by the august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Amin Ali and another Vs. The State” (2011 SCMR 323) where the august Supreme Court of Pakistan was pleased to reject the evidence of injured witnesses and held as under:- “11. All the three witnesses deposed that the deceased had received three injuries, but the Medical Officer found six injuries on the person of the deceased. One of them had blackening. None of the witnesses deposed that any of the appellants had caused the injuries from a close range but on the contrary in the site plan the place of Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 14 firing has been shown 8 feet away from the deceased. Thus from such a distance injury with blackening cannot be caused as it can be caused from a distance of less than 3 feet as per Modi's Medical Jurisprudence.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 14 firing has been shown 8 feet away from the deceased. Thus from such a distance injury with blackening cannot be caused as it can be caused from a distance of less than 3 feet as per Modi's Medical Jurisprudence. The Medical Officer did not show as to which of the injury was entry or exit wound on the person of the deceased. The medical officer stated that metalic projectile was recovered from wound No.1/B which was an exit wound. If it was an exit wound then the metalic projectile would have been out of the body. The presence of metalic projectile in the body clearly establishes the fact that it is not an exit wound but an entry wound. The medical officer has not shown that any of the injuries had inverted or averted margins so as to ascertain as to which of the injuries is entry or exit wound. Thus on this count there is a conflict between the medical and oral evidence. Furthermore, according to Medical Officer, the P.W.15 had four injuries out of them two were entry and two were exit wounds but the P.Ws. 13 and 14 deposed that the injured had received three injuries. Thus the P.Ws. have shown one exit wound as entry wound. With regard to the injured Tanveer Hussain, the Medical Officer showed two injuries one entry wound on the chest and one exit wound on the back but all the three eye-witnesses deposed that P.W.14 had received two injuries on his chest. As regards injuries on the person of Mst. Maqbool Bibi. The Medical Officer found one entry wound on her back with blackening, whereas P.Ws. 13, 14 and 15 deposed that the fire shot was fired from the roof of the shop. Entry wound with blackening marks cannot be caused from such a long distance. From the above position it is manifest that the ocular testimony is in conflict with the medical evidence. Thus, the deceased and injured did not receive the injuries in the manner, as alleged by the prosecution...................................... 13.

From the above position it is manifest that the ocular testimony is in conflict with the medical evidence. Thus, the deceased and injured did not receive the injuries in the manner, as alleged by the prosecution...................................... 13. From the above evidence of the P. Ws., they do not appear to be truthful witnesses; therefore, no implicit reliance can be placed on their evidence.” Furthermore, as mentioned above, according to the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) they had been sitting inside the Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) when the occurrence took place, however the Investigating Officer of the case did not observe or took into possession any cot or chairs which were used by the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) to sit on. Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, visited the place of occurrence, after the occurrence and remained there for a considerable time. During the course of his stay at the place of occurrence, Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 15 Officer of the case, did not take into possession the cots or chairs which were used by the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) alongwith the other recoveries, though there was no occasion for the said chairs or the cots, which were allegedly used by the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) to sit, not to have been present or not being taken into possession by the Investigating Officer or not being observed as present during his visit at the place of occurrence, if the same were available. Shamas-ud-Din, SI (PW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, even was not shown the house/Dera/Baithak of Muhammad Akhtar (PW- 4) during his stay at the place of occurrence or even during the course of investigation, so as to prove that such a house or the Baithak or the Dera of Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) existed where the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were sitting.

Appeal No. 70 of 2018 15 Officer of the case, did not take into possession the cots or chairs which were used by the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) alongwith the other recoveries, though there was no occasion for the said chairs or the cots, which were allegedly used by the prosecution witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) to sit, not to have been present or not being taken into possession by the Investigating Officer or not being observed as present during his visit at the place of occurrence, if the same were available. The question for determination before this Court now is that whether the evidence of the prosecution witnesses which has been disbelieved qua the acquitted co-accused of the appellant can be believed against the appellant. The proposition of law in Criminal Administration of Justice, that a common set of witnesses can be used for recording acquittal and conviction against the accused persons who were charged for the commission of same offence, is now a settled proposition. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan has recently held that partial truth cannot be allowed and perjury is a serious crime. This view stems from the notion that once a witness is found to have lied about a material aspect of a case, it cannot then be safely assumed that the said witness will declare the truth about any other aspect of the case. We have noted that the view should be that "the testimony of one detected in a lie was wholly worthless and must of necessity be rejected." If a witness is not coming out with the whole truth, then his evidence is liable to be discarded as a whole, meaning thereby that his evidence cannot be used either for convicting accused or acquitting some of them facing trial in the same case. This proposition is enshrined in the maxim falsus in uno falsus in omnibus. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in Criminal Miscellaneous Application No. 200 of 2019 in Criminal Appeal No. 238-L of 2013 reported as PLD 2019 Supreme Court 527 has enunciated the following binding principles: - “The Pakistan Penal Code, 1860 (P.P.C.) contains many offences dealing with perjury and giving false testimony. The very fact that there is a whole chapter, numbered XI, dedicated to such offences amply testifies to the fact that matters relating to giving of testimony were taken very seriously by those who drafted the P.P.C. and their continued retention in the P.P.C. ever since reflects the will of the legislature, which is the chosen representative body of the people of Pakistan through which they exercise their authority within the limits prescribed by Almighty Allah. Holding that the rule falsus in uno, falsus in omnibus is inapplicable in this country practically encourages commission of perjury which is a serious offence in this country. A court of law cannot permit something which the law expressly forbids............................................................................................. 21. We may observe in the end that a judicial system which permits deliberate falsehood is doomed to fail and a society which tolerates it is destined to self-destruct. Truth is the foundation of justice and justice is the core and bedrock of a civilized society and, thus, any compromise on truth amounts to a compromise on a society's future as a just, fair and civilized society. Our judicial system has suffered a lot as a consequence of the above mentioned permissible deviation from the truth and it is about time that such a colossal wrong may be rectified in all earnestness. Therefore, in light of the discussion made above, we declare that the rule falsus in uno, falsus in omnibus shall henceforth be an integral part of our jurisprudence in criminal cases and the same shall be given effect to, followed and applied by all the courts in the country in its letter and spirit. It is also directed that a witness found by a court to have resorted to a deliberate falsehood on a material aspect shall, without any latitude, invariably be proceeded against for committing perjury.” Both the witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were found to have made false statements with regard to Muhammad Amir, Muhammad Ijaz, Sajjad Ahmad, Muhammad Dilshad and Allah Ditta (all since acquitted), the co-accused of the appellant.

It is also directed that a witness found by a court to have resorted to a deliberate falsehood on a material aspect shall, without any latitude, invariably be proceeded against for committing perjury.” Both the witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) were found to have made false statements with regard to Muhammad Amir, Muhammad Ijaz, Sajjad Ahmad, Muhammad Dilshad and Allah Ditta (all since acquitted), the co-accused of the appellant. We are unable to find any independent corroboration of the prosecution case against the appellant. We find no reason to believe the statements of the witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) with regard to the appellant in absence of any reason to do so. This lying on part of the witnesses namely Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW-4) with regard to Muhammad Amir, Muhammad Ijaz, Sajjad Ahmad, Muhammad Dilshad and Allah Ditta (all since acquitted), the co- accused of the appellant, has vitiated our trust in them. We are thus satisfied that the evidence of Khuda Bakhsh (PW-3) and Muhammad Akhtar (PW- Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 38 4) has no worth and deserves outright rejection. It is a settled principle of law that for giving the benefit of the doubt it is not necessary that there should be so many circumstances rather if only a single circumstance creating reasonable doubt in the mind of a prudent person is available then such benefit is to be extended to an accused not as a matter of concession but as of right. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Needless to mention that while giving the benefit of doubt to an accused it is not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubt.

The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Needless to mention that while giving the benefit of doubt to an accused it is not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubt. If there is a circumstance which creates reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of such doubt, not as a matter of grace and concession, but as a matter of right. It is based on the maxim, "it is better that ten guilty persons be acquitted rather than one innocent person be convicted". Reliance in this behalf can be made upon the cases of Tariq Pervez v. The State (1995 SCMR 1345), Ghulam Qadir and 2 others v. The State (2008 SCMR 1221), Muhammad Akramv.The State (2009 SCMR 230) and Muhammad Zamanv.The State (2014 SCMR 749).” Reliance is also placed on the judgment of the august Supreme Court of Pakistan Muhammad Akram Vs. the State (2009 SCMR 230) in which it has been observed in paragraph No.13 of page 236 as infra: Murder Reference No. 04 of 2018 Crl. Appeal No. 70 of 2018 39 “It is an axiomatic principle of law that in case of doubt, the benefit thereof must accrue in favour of the accused as matter of right and not of grace.
